title: Ingress — Hostname wildcards
text:
Hosts can be precise matches (for example “
foo.bar.com
”) or a wildcard (for
example “
*.foo.com
”). Precise matches require that the HTTP
host
header
matches the
host
field. Wildcard matches require the HTTP
host
header is
equal to the suffix of the wildcard rule.
Host
Host header
Match?
*.foo.com
bar.foo.com
Matches based on shared suffix
*.foo.com
baz.bar.foo.com
No match, wildcard only covers a single DNS label
*.foo.com
foo.com
No match, wildcard only covers a single DNS label
